reforms.description: The federal government has refocused the National Research Council (NRC) on business-oriented research to better align public research with private sector needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Aligning public research with business needs is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The NRC has been refocused to support business-oriented research.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the focus of public research towards business needs, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The government has refocused the NRC on business-oriented research.

reforms.description: The 2012 budget proposed to double funding to small and medium-sized businesses through the Industrial Research Assistance Program (IRAP), enhancing support for innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing funding for small and medium-sized enterprises is expected to stimulate innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Funding to small and medium-sized businesses through IRAP is being doubled immediately.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding increase is a significant enhancement of support for innovation among small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.source_quote: Funding to small and medium-sized businesses through IRAP is being doubled immediately.

reforms.description: The federal government has proposed significant streamlining of regulatory approvals for major infrastructure projects, which is expected to enhance the efficiency of project implementation and support innovation in various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory delays, the initiative is expected to facilitate faster project completion, thereby enhancing productivity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal budget proposed significant streamlining of regulatory approvals.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses a critical bottleneck in infrastructure development, which is essential for supporting broader economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The recent federal budget proposes significant streamlining of regulatory approvals for major infrastructure projects.

reforms.description: The federal government has provided substantial funding to clean energy projects and sustainable agriculture, aiming to foster innovation in these sectors while addressing environmental challenges.
reforms.package_name: 2012 Clean Energy and Agriculture Funding Initiative
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in clean energy and sustainable agriculture is expected to drive innovation, create jobs, and contribute to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government has accorded substantial funding to clean energy projects and sustainable agriculture.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding initiative is significant for promoting innovation in key sectors that are vital for economic sustainability and environmental protection.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has accorded substantial funding to clean energy projects and sustainable agriculture.

reforms.description: The Canadian government has made changes to immigration policies to emphasize language skills and improve outcomes for skilled immigrants, including initiatives to provide employers with guidelines for assessing foreign credentials.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the skills of immigrants contributes to knowledge diffusion and improves the overall skill level of the workforce, which is essential for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has launched several initiatives to improve immigrant outcomes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses a significant issue in the labor market by improving the integration of skilled immigrants, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: Canadian governments have also launched several initiatives in recent years to provide employers with guidelines for assessing foreign credentials and hiring internationally trained workers.

reforms.description: The Canadian Experience Class was introduced to facilitate permanent residency for foreign students who have studied in Canada and gained work experience, aiming to retain skilled graduates.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By retaining skilled graduates, the initiative enhances the talent pool available for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Canadian Experience Class was introduced in 2008 to facilitate the attainment of permanent resident status for foreign students.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly addresses the retention of skilled individuals who can contribute to innovation and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: In 2008, Citizenship and Immigration Canada introduced the Canadian Experience Class, which facilitates the attainment of permanent resident status for foreign students.

reforms.description: The Canadian government has proposed a system of business vouchers to encourage firms to contract for R&D with academics and other businesses, aiming to enhance technology transfer.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing firms to engage in R&D, the voucher system is expected to enhance collaboration between academia and industry, leading to increased innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal for a system of business vouchers was mentioned as a means to improve technology transfer.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift in how businesses can access R&D resources, potentially leading to improved innovation outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: A system of business vouchers (in lieu of tax credits) to purchase or contract for R&D with both academics and other business could help to focus more research on business needs.

reforms.description: The federal government is making targeted improvements to the Investment Canada Act to enhance transparency while preserving investor confidentiality, which includes creating procedures for timely and binding opinions on compliance for foreign investors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing transparency in foreign investment can attract more capital and expertise, fostering innovation and competitiveness in the Canadian economy.
reforms.status_evidence: As announced in the 2012 budget, the federal government is in the process of making targeted improvements to the Investment Canada Act.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms aim to improve the investment climate, which is significant for attracting foreign capital and enhancing innovation capacity, though they are not a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government is in the process of making targeted improvements to the Investment Canada Act to enhance transparency while preserving investor confidentiality.

reforms.description: The federal government plans to target more active investment in Canadian growth companies and innovative entrepreneurs under the Business Immigration Program, recognizing the economic growth benefits from skilled immigration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting skilled immigrants who are likely to be innovative entrepreneurs, the program aims to stimulate economic growth and enhance the startup ecosystem in Canada.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government outlined that it will begin to target more active investment in Canadian growth companies and more innovative entrepreneurs under the Business Immigration Program.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to enhance the entrepreneurial landscape in Canada, although it is not a comprehensive reform of the immigration system.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government outlined that it will begin to target more active investment in Canadian growth companies and more innovative entrepreneurs under the Business Immigration Program.

reforms.description: The Canadian government proposed to reduce the general SR&ED investment tax credit rate from 20% to 15% as part of the 2012 federal budget, aligning it with recent corporate income tax reductions.
reforms.component_name: Reduction of SR&ED Tax Credit Rate
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While the reduction aims to streamline the tax system, it may discourage R&D investment among larger firms, potentially leading to a decrease in overall R&D activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The 2012 federal budget proposed the reduction in the SR&ED tax credit rate.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the tax treatment of R&D, affecting a large portion of business R&D funding in Canada.
reforms.source_quote: The 2012 federal budget proposes, as of 2014, a reduction in the general SR&ED investment tax credit rate from 20% to 15%.

reforms.description: The 2012 federal budget proposed to double the contributions of the Industrial Research Assistance Program (IRAP) to small and medium-sized businesses, enhancing support for R&D and innovative activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for IRAP is expected to stimulate R&D and innovation among small and medium-sized enterprises, enhancing their competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The 2012 federal budget doubled the programme’s contributions to small and medium-sized businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances an existing program, providing more resources to support innovation in small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.source_quote: The 2012 federal budget doubled the programme’s contributions to small and medium-sized businesses.

reforms.description: The Jenkins report recommended using savings from streamlining the small-firm SR&ED to expand the Industrial Research Assistance Program (IRAP) and commercialisation vouchers, while consolidating smaller programmes into distinct product lines.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding IRAP and commercialisation vouchers is expected to enhance support for innovation and R&D activities in small firms, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Jenkins report provided recommendations for expanding IRAP and consolidating programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed changes represent a significant overhaul of existing funding mechanisms for innovation, aiming to streamline support and enhance effectiveness across the innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The Jenkins report... recommended using the savings from streamlining the small-firm SR&ED to expand IRAP and commercialisation vouchers.

reforms.description: The Jenkins panel report supported using public procurement to bolster innovation, particularly for SMEs, by framing procurement tenders in terms of needs to be met rather than detailed specifications.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By encouraging innovative solutions through public procurement, the policy aims to stimulate market demand for new technologies and services, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Jenkins panel report supported using public procurement to bolster innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public procurement can be leveraged to support innovation, particularly for small and medium enterprises.
reforms.source_quote: The Jenkins panel report supported using public procurement to bolster innovation, particularly for SMEs.

reforms.description: The Canadian government has made available an extra CAD 500 million in funding to support venture capital, including CAD 100 million through the BDC and an additional CAD 400 million in new funding, aimed at catalyzing the private VC and equity industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding is expected to stimulate the venture capital market, encouraging investment in innovative firms and enhancing their growth potential.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal budget allocated additional funding for VC support.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment aimed at revitalizing the venture capital market, which is crucial for supporting innovative startups and enhancing overall economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The 2012 federal budget made available an extra CAD 500 million in funding to VC support, including CAD 100 million through the BDC and an additional CAD 400 million in new funding.

reforms.description: The Canadian government has removed major tax barriers to private equity, including narrowing the definition of taxable Canadian property, which eliminates the need for tax reporting of dispositions by non-residents of many equity investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing tax barriers, this reform is expected to attract more private equity investment, thereby enhancing the capital available for innovative firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government has removed major tax barriers to private equity.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant structural change aimed at enhancing the attractiveness of the Canadian market for private equity, which is vital for funding innovative ventures.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has removed major tax barriers to private equity.

reforms.description: The Canadian government is pushing for a marginal rebalancing away from basic research towards more commercially relevant research, incentivizing academics to produce research with immediate applications.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning research more closely with business needs, the reform aims to enhance productivity and innovation in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is seeking to incentivize commercially relevant research.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in research funding priorities, which could have lasting impacts on the innovation landscape in Canada.
reforms.source_quote: To bolster this process, academics should face stronger incentives to produce commercially relevant and creative research.

reforms.description: The Competition Policy Review Panel suggested that Canadian universities could expedite the transfer of IP rights by adopting an 'innovator ownership' model, inspired by successful commercialization practices.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating IP transfer can enhance collaboration between academia and industry, leading to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The panel suggested moving to an 'innovator ownership' model to improve IP rights transfer.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform could significantly enhance the efficiency of technology transfer from universities to the market, impacting innovation outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The Competition Policy Review Panel suggested that Canada’s tertiary education institutions could expedite the transfer of IP rights by moving to an 'innovator ownership' model.

reforms.description: The Canadian government announced a reallocation of CAD60 million over three years to fund the development of digital skills among youth and Aboriginal groups, promoting enrolment in key STEM disciplines.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital skills is expected to improve employability and innovation capacity in the workforce, aligning education with market needs.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal budget allocated funds for the development of digital skills.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in human capital aimed at addressing skills shortages in key areas, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The 2011 federal budget announced that HRSDC would reallocate CAD60 million over three years to fund the development of 'digital skills' among youth and Aboriginal groups.

reforms.description: The Canada Foundation for Innovation (CFI) was established to fund infrastructure costs for research projects in Canadian universities, colleges, research hospitals, and non-profit research institutions, providing up to 40% of funding for these initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By funding research infrastructure, the CFI enhances the capacity for research and innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The CFI has been operational since its establishment in 1997.
reforms.importance_rationale: The CFI represents a significant structural change in funding research infrastructure, which is crucial for enhancing Canada's research capacity and innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The CFI funds infrastructure costs for research projects in Canadian universities, colleges, research hospitals and non-profit research institutions.

reforms.description: The Canada Research Chairs programme was created to provide funding for outstanding researchers, enhancing Canada's research capacity and attracting top talent to Canadian institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting and funding top researchers, the programme enhances the quality of research and innovation, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been operational since its creation in 2000.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Canada Research Chairs programme is a major initiative aimed at enhancing research capacity in Canada, significantly impacting the quality and output of research.
reforms.source_quote: The Canada Research Chairs programme provides funding of CAD200000 annually for seven years to 1000 Tier-1 chairs selected for outstanding research contributions.

reforms.description: The Indirect Costs programme was introduced to help universities and colleges fund a portion of the indirect costs associated with federally funded research projects, addressing a critical gap in research funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing funding for indirect costs, the programme supports the sustainability of research activities, thereby enhancing overall research output and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been operational since its introduction in 2003.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important, the Indirect Costs programme is a targeted initiative that addresses specific funding gaps rather than a broad structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: The Indirect Costs programme provides annual grants to universities and colleges to help fund a portion of indirect institutional costs of federally funded research projects.

reforms.description: The Centres of Excellence in Commercialisation and Research programme (CECR) was launched to advance research and assist in the commercialisation of new technologies, products, and services, focusing on priority areas of the federal government's science and technology strategy.
reforms.component_name: Centres of Excellence in Commercialisation and Research programme
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the commercialisation of research, the CECR programme enhances the translation of research into marketable innovations, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been operational since its launch in 2007.
reforms.importance_rationale: The CECR programme represents a significant investment in the commercialisation of research, which is crucial for enhancing innovation and economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: The CECR is investing CAD285 million over five years in centres to advance research and assist the commercialisation of new technologies, products and services.

reforms.description: The Canada Excellence Research Chairs (CERC) programme was created to attract world-renowned researchers to establish research programmes at Canadian universities, significantly enhancing the country's research capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting top researchers, the CERC programme enhances the quality of research and innovation, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been operational since its establishment in 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: The CERC programme is a major initiative aimed at enhancing research capacity in Canada, significantly impacting the quality and output of research.
reforms.source_quote: The CERC awards world-renowned researchers and their teams up to CAD10 million over seven years to establish research programmes at Canadian universities.

reforms.description: The federal government announced funding and appointed an advisory panel to develop an international education strategy aimed at enhancing Canada's global competitiveness in higher education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing international education can attract skilled individuals and foster innovation through diverse perspectives and ideas.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government announced funding and appointed an advisory panel.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy aims to improve Canada's position in the global education market, which is significant for attracting talent and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In 2011, the federal government announced funding and appointed an advisory panel to develop an international education strategy.

reforms.description: Québec introduced 'performance commitments' that tie tuition and public funding increases to the attainment of specific objectives based on 13 target indicators.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While it aims to improve institutional performance, it may also create perverse incentives that stifle innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Québec has introduced 'performance commitments' for funding increases.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy, linking financial support to performance metrics, which can influence institutional behavior.
reforms.source_quote: Québec has introduced 'performance commitments', which formally designate all tuition and public funding increases to universities to meet objectives based on the attainment of 13 target indicators.

reforms.description: The Canadian government aims to boost innovation to enhance productivity growth, focusing on improving knowledge flows to businesses and strengthening commercialization processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing innovation is expected to raise productivity, which is crucial for sustaining living standards.
reforms.status_evidence: Innovation is high on the government’s agenda.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in focus towards enhancing innovation as a means to improve productivity, which is critical for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Boosting innovation can raise historically weak productivity growth to sustain living standards.

reforms.description: The government plans to unify the tax subsidy rate for small domestic firms at the lower rate applicable to large firms to encourage scale and innovation adoption.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning tax incentives, firms are encouraged to scale up, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The higher current tax subsidy rate for small domestic firms should be unified at the lower large firm rate.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to streamline tax incentives, which can enhance the innovation landscape for small firms.
reforms.source_quote: The higher current tax subsidy rate for small domestic firms should be unified at the lower large firm rate to encourage firms to attain the scale needed to adopt innovations.

reforms.description: The Canadian government is shifting its funding for tertiary education institutions (TEIs) away from core operating activities towards research, emphasizing the role of TEIs in contributing to innovation through knowledge creation and skills development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing funding for research, the policy aims to enhance innovation capacity and improve the skills of graduates, which can lead to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy emphasis on innovation has been noted as a shift in government funding priorities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in funding strategy that could enhance the research capabilities of TEIs, thereby contributing to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Increasing policy emphasis on innovation has generally shifted government funding for TEIs away from core operating activities towards research.

reforms.description: The federal government has committed to reconfiguring the National Research Council (NRC) to focus more on demand-driven applied research that better serves the needs of businesses, addressing the commercialisation gap between academic and applied research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning research efforts with business needs, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government has committed to carrying this reconfiguration further in its new budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the focus of a major public research institution, aiming to bridge the gap between academic research and business innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Jenkins report recommended that the National Research Council (NRC) be reconfigured to be more focused on demand-driven applied research better able to serve the needs of business.

reforms.description: The federal budget announced a study of contingency fees charged by tax preparers for the SR&ED tax incentive program, which is designed to support R&D activities in Canada.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While the reform aims to reduce compliance costs for small firms, it may also introduce uncertainties in the administration of the tax credit.
reforms.status_evidence: The 2012 budget announced a study of contingency fees charged by tax preparers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant issue in the administration of a key innovation funding mechanism, potentially improving access for small firms.
reforms.source_quote: It is estimated that small firms spend on average 14% of their tax credit in compliance costs.
